purpose: Reference sensitive_path_patterns set for EPHEMERAL Tariff (§4.4 R8.F2) with Git, CI/CD, supply-chain, secrets, and IaC coverage. NOT a conformance test vector suite — this is a curated default operators embed (or adapt) into their signed Tariff.
audit_trail: Phase 4 Review Swarm (security-reviewer, architect, code-reviewer, Explore) identified Sec-N5 as operator-configuration gap. Research pass (2026-04-18) consulted GitHub/GitLab branch-protection conventions, OWASP 2025 A03 Software Supply Chain Failures, and documented 2018-2026 supply-chain incidents (xz-utils CVE-2024-3094, tj-actions CVE-2025-30066, SpotBugs/reviewdog chain, Shai-Hulud 2025, axios 2026, event-stream 2018, ua-parser-js 2021, node-ipc 2022, Codecov 2021). Second security-review pass (2026-04-18) added: (a) `**/action.yml` / `**/action.yaml` MUST for GitHub reusable-action attack surface (SpotBugs/reviewdog rationale), (b) Bun ecosystem (`bun.lockb`, `bunfig.toml`) and depth-aware `**/.npmrc` MUST, (c) `pip.conf` / `.pip/pip.conf` / `pip.ini` MUST for pip registry-override pivot, (d) GitLab include-local fragments (`ci/**`, `pipelines/**`) SHOULD, (e) generic `Makefile` / `GNUmakefile` SHOULD extending xz-utils rationale, (f) SOPS-encrypted file variants (`*.sops.*`, `*.enc.*`) SHOULD, (g) lowercase ArgoCD variants + AppProject SHOULD, (h) Swift SPM and Dart/Flutter ecosystems SHOULD, (i) narrowed the `!**/examples/**` whitelist exception to documentation-only variants to prevent defeat of `.env` MUST rule, (j) narrowed `!**/vendor/**` to ecosystem-specific subtrees, (k) clarified `~DEFAULT_BRANCH` fork-PR resolution semantics, (l) documented `refs/heads/release/*` nested-branch limitation.
license_note: Public-domain reference. Customers copy-and-adapt without attribution requirement.

path_patterns.syntax: gitignore/CODEOWNERS-style globs with double-star directory crossing

semantics.!pattern: negation / whitelist exception (processed AFTER all MUST/SHOULD matches)

ref_patterns.syntax: Git refspec with explicit refs/ prefix + fnmatch (no ** directory crossing)
ref_patterns.limitation_on_nested_release_branches: A pattern like `refs/heads/release/*` matches only one path segment. Multi-level naming such as `refs/heads/release/2026/hotfix-123` is NOT matched. Operators with nested release hierarchies SHOULD either flatten to single-segment names or add explicit patterns per depth level (`refs/heads/release/*/*`, `refs/heads/release/*/*/*`). The `**` directory-crossing operator is intentionally NOT supported in ref_patterns to keep Git refspec semantics.

sentinels.~DEFAULT_BRANCH: Resolves to repository default-branch ref at Tariff-issuance time (per §4.4 R8.F6 frozen-at-issuance semantics). For fork-based pull-request workflows (e.g., GitHub pull_request_target, GitLab merge-request-from-fork), the sentinel resolves to the TARGET-repository's default branch — NOT the fork's — because the Tariff is issued against the target repository's identity. Operators with multi-target Tariffs SHOULD author one Tariff per target or use explicit branch names.

regex_escape_hatch.usage: ONLY when glob grammar cannot express the constraint. Implementations SHOULD reject patterns with ReDoS-vulnerable constructs (catastrophic backtracking).

classifications.MUST: Industry consensus + documented supply-chain incidents. Omission creates known attack surface. Every Tariff SHOULD embed this set unchanged or explicitly justify omission in audit trail.
classifications.SHOULD: Context-dependent. Applies when the integration/project uses the named toolchain. Operators evaluate applicability; non-applicable patterns MAY be omitted without audit justification.
classifications.WHITELIST: Explicit exceptions (documentation/test fixtures) that would otherwise trigger false positives. Processed after MUST/SHOULD matches.

categories.title: Git protected-branch and tag refs
categories.rationale: Force-push / ref-delete / tag-overwrite on these refs enables code-injection into protected release paths. Sec-N5 original driver: the pre-v2 default {prod/*, root/*, admin/*, ceremony/*} missed Git ref operations entirely.

patterns.notes: Legacy default; still present in ~40% of repos
git-flow integration branch — applies where git-flow is used
Version tags; overwrite = supply-chain injection via re-tagging
Portable reference to whatever default branch is frozen-at-issuance in Tariff.integration_config.default_branch (§4.4 R8.F6)

categories.title: CI/CD pipeline and workflow files
categories.rationale: Direct attack surface. CVE-2025-30066 (tj-actions/changed-files, 23k repos compromised 2025-03), SpotBugs→reviewdog chain (Nov 2024–Mar 2025), Codecov 2021. Workflow-file modification is equivalent to build-time code execution.

patterns.notes: Reusable action root — SpotBugs/reviewdog 2024-25 attack surface (any depth)
GitLab include-local / generic CI fragments outside .gitlab/

categories.title: Build system and container specifications
categories.rationale: CVE-2024-3094 (xz-utils, 2024-03) exploited autotools plumbing (`build-to-host.m4`) — a file most defenses ignore. Dockerfile compromise injects trojanized base images into every downstream build.

patterns.notes: Non-autotools projects — xz CVE-2024-3094 rationale extends

categories.title: Package/dependency manifests and lockfiles
categories.rationale: Primary supply-chain pivot point. Incidents: event-stream (2018), ua-parser-js (2021), node-ipc (2022), Shai-Hulud 1+2 (2025, 500+ packages), axios (2026). Lockfile-version-pin bypass is a documented 2026 attack vector.

python.notes: pip registry override — supply-chain pivot analogous to .npmrc

jvm.notes: Binary wrapper — OWASP 2025 A03 explicit callout

categories.title: Secret files, private keys, credential stores
categories.rationale: Direct exposure. GitGuardian Top-10 leaking-extensions statistics: 2023-2026 incidents consistently show .env, *.pem, kubeconfig as top-3 leak vectors. Tier bump is +2 (not +1) because exposure is immediate and not bounded by revocation latency.

patterns.notes: Armored secrets — unless sealed via SOPS/sealed-secrets

categories.title: Infrastructure-as-Code and deployment manifests
categories.rationale: Config-mutation attack path. Terraform state files (*.tfstate) contain live credentials per AWS Prescriptive Guidance. K8s/Helm values files control runtime capability grants. Classification is SHOULD (not MUST) because applicability depends on project layout.

flux_argocd.notes: Lowercase variant — common when rendered by Helm/kustomize

categories.title: Original pre-R8 defaults (preserved for backwards compatibility)
categories.rationale: Preserved from the pre-Sec-N5 default set {prod/*, root/*, admin/*, ceremony/*}. Operators already depending on these patterns retain coverage.

whitelist_exceptions.purpose: Documented false-positive exceptions. Processed AFTER all MUST/SHOULD matches via !pattern negation in operator Tariff.

patterns.notes: Narrow exception — covers documentation only. Do NOT whitelist the whole examples/ tree (would defeat .env MUST rule on scaffolding .env files).
Node vendored deps — not the project's own source

whitelist_exceptions.note: Operators SHOULD audit that whitelist exceptions are genuinely documentation or test fixtures. A whitelist match on a real secret file is an audit-review finding. Pre-v1.1 versions had a blanket `!**/vendor/**` exception that incorrectly whitelisted `vendor/modules.txt` (Go) and `vendor/**/*.tf` (Terraform); narrowed in v1.0.0 to ecosystem-specific vendor subtrees.

exploited_files: CI pipeline bash uploader → env exfil

false_positive_hotspots.guidance: Patterns known to match too broadly. Avoid as-is; scope tightly.

entries.problem: Matches fixtures, OpenAPI specs, i18n files
Matches any file named 'config' including docs

entries.replacement: Acceptable risk for simplicity; document if test Dockerfiles should be excluded

tariff_embedding_guidance.integration_into_tariff: Customers embed selected patterns into their signed Tariff as sensitive_path_patterns field (§4.4 R8.F2). The Tariff field is a SET-typed array of glob strings (R7.C6 §4.2.1). Structured metadata in this reference (classification, rationale, incident_refs) is operator-facing only — NOT included in the signed Tariff payload.

minimum_tariff_embedding.description: Minimum viable Tariff coverage per Sec-N5. Adopting ALL 'MUST' patterns from categories git_refs, ci_cd_workflows, build_and_container_files, dependency_manifests, secrets_and_credentials, legacy_original_defaults satisfies the pre-production requirement.
minimum_tariff_embedding.approximate_pattern_count_note: Counts verified 2026-04-18 post-review-swarm. Post-v1.0 internal security-review pass added ~30 patterns across action.yml, Bun, pip.conf, GitLab include-local, generic Makefile, SOPS, ArgoCD lowercase, Swift, and Dart/Flutter coverage before ship.

tariff_embedding_guidance.customization: Operators MUST review every pattern against their project layout. A SHOULD-classified pattern that doesn't apply (e.g., `mix.lock` in a non-Elixir project) MAY be omitted. A MUST-classified pattern SHOULD NOT be omitted without explicit audit-trail justification.
tariff_embedding_guidance.update_cadence: This reference artifact is spec-version-pinned (round8-delta-applied). Updates ship with subsequent spec revisions. Incident-driven interim additions MAY be published as advisory updates without spec revision — operators SHOULD subscribe to EPHEMERAL advisory channel.

conformance_cross_reference.note: This file is NOT a conformance test vector suite. For test vectors exercising R8.F2 sensitive-path bump behavior, see:

related_vectors.coverage: R8.F2 prod/* and root/* bump; NFKC homoglyph bypass

conformance_cross_reference.phase_b_expansion: Phase B validator harness SHOULD author additional fuzz-baseline vectors exercising ci_cd_workflows, dependency_manifests, and secrets_and_credentials coverage. Tracked as Sec-N5 Phase B instrumentation task.
